title: elymus villosus
intro: Elymus villosus, also known as the hairy wheatgrass or hairy wildrye, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Elymus villosus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, Elymus villosus has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal medicine to make salves and creams for topical application.
